message:
fix(safety): bounded buffer caps for parser, tmux, ssh, kitty [wa-3kxe]

Add hard limits to prevent unbounded memory growth across several
subsystems that accept untrusted or high-volume input:

escape-parser (XtGetTcap):
- MAX_TCAP_NAMES (512): cap on accumulated DCS +q names per sequence
- MAX_TCAP_CURRENT_BYTES (1 MiB): cap on per-name hex payload size
- GetTcapBuilder gains `discarding` flag; once cap is hit, further
  data is silently discarded until the DCS terminates
- Two new tests verify parser recovery after overlong payloads

tmux (backlog + command queue):
- MAX_BACKLOG_BYTES_PER_PANE (1 MiB): cap_backlog_payload() truncates
  to tail bytes, preserving most recent output
- CMD_QUEUE_WARNING_DEPTH (10,000): log warning when command queue
  depth suggests protocol churn or a stalled consumer
- Three new tests for cap_backlog_payload (small, over-limit, exact)

tmux_commands (pane state cleanup):
- Extract remove_tmux_pane_state_entries() helper that atomically
  removes pane entries from both remote_panes and backlog maps
- Refactor remove_detached_pane() and remove_detached_window() to
  release locks before calling into Mux, preventing potential deadlock
- Add stale window pruning in list-windows handler: windows no longer
  in the active session are cleaned up automatically
- New test for remove_tmux_pane_state_entries correctness

ssh (descriptor buffer):
- MAX_DESCRIPTOR_BUF_SIZE (4 MiB): read_into_buf() stops reading when
  buffer is full, providing natural backpressure to the SSH channel
  until the write side drains data

kitty image state:
- MAX_KITTY_ACCUMULATOR_CHUNKS (4,096): accumulate_chunk() discards
  all fragments when cap is exceeded, preventing unbounded growth from
  malformed multi-chunk transfers that never send a final chunk
- MAX_KITTY_NUMBER_TO_ID_ENTRIES (4,096): enforce_limits() evicts
  stale number_to_id entries after ID removal and caps total entries
- remove_data_for_id() now also prunes number_to_id mappings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
